_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in developing crisis action timelines
reference_document_element_description: Skill in designing and maintaining a model risk governance framework, including policy development, role and responsibility definition, escalation protocols, exception management, and board-level model risk reporting.
comments: NICE skill intersects with WIDAI RM domain. Skill RM-S-006 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RM-S-008 (RM, cosine: 0.457), LS-S-007 (LS, cosine: 0.444).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: Moderate similarity (4.1/10). The cross-encoder finds meaningful but bounded overlap — consistent with cross-framework mapping between cybersecurity and data/AI domains where shared governance concepts use different terminology.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: Moderate token-level alignment (F1=0.884). Precision exceeds Recall (P=0.907 > R=0.861), indicating the NICE element's semantic content is more tightly aligned with the KSA than the reverse.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: Contradiction-dominant (77.6%). The model detects scope divergence — the concepts may use similar vocabulary in opposing contexts. This warrants review but may reflect domain-specific usage patterns rather than true semantic conflict.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder (0.467) exceeds STS (0.409). The independent embeddings find more similarity than cross-attention, suggesting surface-level vocabulary overlap exceeds the deeper semantic relationship.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Low lexical overlap (7.4%). Limited shared vocabulary — the relationship is primarily semantic rather than terminological.
